findings.evidence: grep hit: `Hermitian.lean:257:  change ω.hermitianForm J v w = _`; Mathlib defines `InnerProductSpace.Core.toInner'` through `cd.toInner` in `Analysis/InnerProductSpace/Defs.lean:448-449`.
findings.fix: Add a short comment explaining why this projection defeq is intended and stable here, or prove the formula through an explicit projection/helper lemma for `hermitianCore.inner`.
findings.issue: `hermitianCore_inner` uses an undocumented `change` to replace `@inner ℂ V h.hermitianCore.toInner v w` by `ω.hermitianForm J v w`, relying on definitional equality through `InnerProductSpace.Core`/`toInner` packaging.

findings.evidence: grep hit: `Hermitian.lean:179-184` proves `hJv`; existing lemma is `CompatibleMetric.lean:83: lemma associatedBilinForm_skewAdjoint (hinv : ω.Invariant J) (v w : V) : ω.associatedBilinForm J (J v) w = -ω.associatedBilinForm J v (J w)`.
findings.fix: Replace the local `hJv` proof with `hinv.associatedBilinForm_skewAdjoint v w` rewritten by `associatedBilinForm_apply`, or add a direct reusable lemma if that exact unbundled form is needed repeatedly.
findings.issue: `hermitianForm_smul_left_aux` reproves the skew-adjoint identity `ω (J v) w = -ω v (J w)` by a local `calc` plus `linarith`, making the proof longer and more brittle than the existing API fact.

summary: Two proof-quality issues remain: one public simp lemma relies on undocumented wrapper definitional equality, and one proof reproves an existing geometric lemma inline.
